target.title: feat(ui): improve control UI chat and task UX
target.body:
## Summary
Focused PR extracting the UI improvements from the stalled #65258. The original PR had fallen 10,598 commits behind main.

### Changes
- **Grouped chat rendering** with tool card summaries and collapse/expand
- **Tool cards** with timeline view, raw output toggle, and canvas preview in sidebar
- **Exec-approval view** with improved layout and states  
- **Markdown sidebar** with rich content display
- **Chat view** improvements with grouped message rendering
- **App-render and app-gateway** improvements for better chat UX
- **Login gate** component
- **App-view-state** for improved chat/task UI state management

### Files changed (24 UI files)
- `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` + test
- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`
- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` + test
- `ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.ts` + test
- `ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.ts`, `markdown-sidebar.ts`
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts`, `app-render.helpers.ts`, `app.ts`
- `ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts` + node test
- `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts` + node test
- `ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts`, `sidebar-content.ts`
- `ui/src/ui/types/chat-types.ts`
- CSS: `ui/src/styles/chat/{grouped,layout,tool-cards}.css`, `components.css`, `layout.css`

### Note
Skipped `chat-spaces.ts` (network timeout fetching from original PR commit).

### UI Proof / Mockup Preview
![openclaw_ui_preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aayushprsingh/openclaw/ui/chat-task-ux-improvements/assets/openclaw_ui_preview.png)

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping through the shared speech-core preparation path before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.
- Reviewer focus: config schema/docs, the shared TTS preprocessing path, and the empty-after-strip guard.

Out of scope: changing default TTS behavior, validating every vendor-specific pronunciation path, or adding provider-specific filtering.

## Linked context

- Related: #77969.
- This was opened as a focused external fix for noisy TTS output when emoji and decorative symbols are spoken by providers.
- No maintainer-only deployment, credential, or production environment change is required.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch tts-skip-v3, commit 06545b841d08, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
  - node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS
  - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  - pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
- Evidence after fix:

      $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
      # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
      # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
      TS
      {
        "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
        "commit": "06545b841d08",
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
        "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
        "observed": [
          {
            "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
            "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
            "success": true,
            "provider": "microsoft",
            "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
            "target": "audio-file",
            "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
            "bytes": 39456,
            "headerHex": "fff364c4"
          },
          {
            "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
            "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
            "success": true,
            "provider": "microsoft",
            "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
            "target": "audio-file",
            "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
            "bytes": 36288,
            "headerHex": "fff364c4"
          }
        ],
        "emptyAfterStrip": {
          "success": false,
          "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
          "provider": null
        },
        "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
      }

      $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
      ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

      $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
      Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests       32 passed (32)

      $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
      Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests       14 passed (14)

      $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
      exit_code=0

- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing ✓, ©, and 🫠; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: ffprobe was not installed in the proof container, so artifact validation used nonzero byte sizes and MP3 frame headers. The live proof used the credential-free Microsoft path rather than paid or account-bound providers.
- Before evidence: not captured in this PR body; the regression was that emoji/symbol text reached synthesis without a shared opt-in strip step and empty-after-strip requests could still reach provider setup paths.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for the behavioral change:

    node --import tsx - <<'TS' ...real Microsoft speech-provider proof...
    ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
    pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod

Regression coverage added or updated:

- extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
- src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts

Locked-in scenarios:

- file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
- stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
- telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
- telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
- tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

Template repair validation on 2026-05-27:

    Authenticated GitHub API read of the PR base template and PR body.
    Authenticated GitHub API update of this PR body.
    Authenticated GitHub API re-read verified these current template headings are present:
    Summary, Linked context, Real behavior proof (required for external PRs), Tests and validation, Risk checklist, Current review state.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, but only when messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols is explicitly enabled.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: Yes, a new optional config field is added; no migration is needed and unset/false preserves existing behavior.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: Unicode filtering might remove characters a user expected to hear, or filtering might diverge between TTS paths.
- Mitigation: the option is disabled by default, filtering is centralized in the shared speech-core preparation path, and regression tests cover file, stream, telephony, empty-after-strip, and adjacent tool behavior.

Compatibility notes:

- Backward compatible: Yes.
- Config/env changes: Yes, optional messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols.
- Migration needed: No.
- Upgrade steps: none unless a user wants to opt into emoji/symbol stripping.

## Current review state

- Next action: resolve the separate upstream/main generated config metadata conflicts for this branch, then let maintainers and automation continue review.
- Still waiting on author/maintainer/CI/external proof: author-side branch conflict refresh remains tracked separately; no additional external proof is being added in this body-only update.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: this body update repairs the current-template gap by replacing the older long-form PR body headings with every current upstream template heading and concrete content under each one.

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache provider-normalized tool schema `parameters` for repeated embedded agent turns when the active provider hook exposes a stable hook-owned cache key.
- Keep the cache conservative: the key includes provider hook identity plus provider/model/runtime inputs and ordered original tool schema signatures; cached values clone only tool `parameters`, not tool objects, execute closures, approval state, or guards.
- Add `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` as a kill switch, SDK docs/API surface for `resolveToolSchemaCacheKey`, and focused cache-key, clone-safety, env-disable, eviction, and runtime-handle coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated embedded agent turns no longer redo provider tool-schema normalization when the provider hook identity, provider/model/config inputs, and ordered tool schema signature are unchanged. Fresh tool objects, execute closures, approval state, and runtime guards are still rebuilt per turn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, current head `4681f705041fd8d8a693393e71d4fc25dcf907b3`, with a local mock OpenAI Responses server and the real `runEmbeddedAgent` OpenAI Responses path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran one real embedded-agent turn against the local mock OpenAI Responses server with `tools.allow: ["exec", "read"]`; the mock model requested the real OpenClaw `exec` tool with `command: "printf OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_TOOL_OK"`, received the tool output through `function_call_output`, then returned `OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_PROOF_OK`. In the same script, reset the provider tool schema cache and called the real `normalizeProviderToolSchemas` twice with matching schema signatures and different execute closures. Also reran the local file-safety CI shard after fixing the watch-node fake-child test hang.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "head": "4681f705041fd8d8a693393e71d4fc25dcf907b3",
  "realRunVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_PROOF_OK",
  "realRunResponseCalls": 2,
  "realRunModelFacingToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "realRunToolOutputs": ["OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_TOOL_OK"],
  "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
  "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true,
  "cacheStatsAfterFirstNormalize": {"bypass": 0, "hit": 0, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1},
  "cacheStatsAfterSecondNormalize": {"bypass": 0, "hit": 1, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1},
  "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true
}
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/watch-node.test.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 1 file / 15 tests

OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-file-safety-include.json OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-runtime-infra-file-safety OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 4 files / 43 tests

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 1 file / 61 tests

git diff --check
exit 0
```

Observed result after fix: the real embedded OpenAI Responses run exposed `exec` and `read` to the model, executed `exec` successfully, returned the tool output to the model as `function_call_output`, and completed the final assistant response. The direct runtime cache assertion then proved the first matching normalization was a miss/store and the second was a hit while preserving the fresh execute closure from the second tool object.

What was not tested: full live-provider latency was not tested. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache complete tool inventory/materialization, policy resolution, prompt inputs, or full agent-turn startup.

target.title: fix(skills): route installs to requested agent workspace [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Routes workspace-affecting skill install/update calls through the existing agent workspace resolver when an explicit `agentId` is provided.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged for callers that do not pass an `agentId`.
- Documents the new workspace selector in the Gateway protocol docs.
- Adds focused regression coverage and a real local Gateway upload-install proof for explicit agent workspaces.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #89743 reports skill loading/install behavior using the fallback workspace instead of the configured workspace.

What is the intended outcome?

- Skill install and ClawHub update operations can target the selected agent workspace instead of always resolving from the default agent.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to skill loading semantics, workspace config defaults, selected-agent UI pass-through, or unrelated skill APIs.

What does success look like?

- Existing callers keep their current behavior, while callers that provide `agentId` get install/update operations routed to that agent workspace.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `agentId` is the intended public Gateway selector for skill mutations.
- Whether selected-agent UI pass-through should be part of this PR or tracked separately.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Related #89743

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

This PR addresses the backend Gateway routing gap for skill install/update. The linked user-facing issue may still need UI/client follow-through if maintainers expect Control UI install calls to pass `agentId`.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer-specific implementation direction yet. I commented on the issue before opening this PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: skill install/update operations could resolve the default agent workspace instead of an explicit/custom agent workspace.
- Real environment tested: Windows 11 local checkout, bundled Node v24.14.0 from the Codex runtime, temporary OpenClaw state/config/workspace directories, and real Gateway upload/install handlers.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' --import tsx scripts\repro\issue-89743-skill-agent-workspace-proof.mts`
  - `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' scripts\run-vitest.mjs exec node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload-agent-workspace.test.ts`
  - `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' scripts\run-vitest.mjs exec node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`
  - `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' scripts\run-vitest.mjs exec node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix:
  - Console output from the local Gateway proof command, with temp paths redacted:
    ```text
    Extracting <temp>\state\tmp\skill-uploads\<upload-id>\archive.zip...
    Installing to <temp>\workspace-writer\skills\proof-upload...
    REAL_GATEWAY_PROOF issue=89743
    flow=skills.upload.begin/chunk/commit -> skills.install source=upload agentId=writer
    install.ok=true
    targetDir=<temp>\workspace-writer\skills\proof-upload
    writerSkillExists=true
    mainSkillExists=false
    writerSkillContainsProofUpload=true
    ```
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload-agent-workspace.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 1 passed (1)`.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 11 passed (11)`.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 10 passed (10)`.
  - `git diff --check` completed with no output.
- Observed result after fix: focused regression tests verify ClawHub install, local dependency install, and ClawHub update use the explicit `writer` agent workspace when `agentId: "writer"` is supplied.
- The real upload-install proof creates temporary `main` and `writer` workspaces, uploads a zip archive through `skills.upload.*`, calls `skills.install` with `agentId: "writer"`, verifies `targetDir` is the writer workspace, verifies `SKILL.md` exists there, and verifies the same skill is absent from the main workspace.
- What was not tested: a full packaged OpenClaw desktop/manual UI workflow or Control UI selected-agent pass-through.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm docs:list` and `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` were blocked by dependency reconciliation/network fetch failures in this Codex worktree, but enough dependencies were present to run the focused Gateway tests above. `codex review --base origin/main` was attempted earlier and failed locally with Windows `Access is denied` for `codex.exe`.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous code resolved install/update workspace paths from `resolveDefaultAgentId(cfg)` instead of the request context.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' scripts\run-vitest.mjs exec node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload-agent-workspace.test.ts`
- `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' scripts\run-vitest.mjs exec node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`
- `& 'C:\Users\aliab\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe' scripts\run-vitest.mjs exec node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added coverage for ClawHub install into an explicit agent workspace.
- Added coverage for local skill dependency install into an explicit agent workspace.
- Added coverage for ClawHub update in an explicit agent workspace.
- Added a real upload-install Gateway proof that installs an uploaded archive into an explicit `writer` agent workspace and verifies it does not land in the default `main` workspace.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The new explicit-workspace regression expectations fail against the old routing because install/update used the default agent workspace resolver.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, for callers that pass the new optional `agentId` field to skill install/update APIs.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Gateway API compatibility and whether install/update callers should select workspaces via `agentId`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The new schema field is optional.
- Existing no-`agentId` behavior falls back through the existing resolver.
- The implementation reuses `resolveSkillsAgentWorkspace`, which nearby skills APIs already use for agent workspace selection.
- Gateway protocol docs now describe the selector and fallback/rejection behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for Ali review before marking ready.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting for CI and maintainer review.
- Ali should review/understand the patch before the PR is marked ready.
- Maintainers should decide whether selected-agent UI/client pass-through belongs in this PR or a follow-up.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed ClawSweeper's docs finding by updating `docs/gateway/protocol.md`.
- Added real Gateway upload-install behavior proof for explicit agent workspace routing.

AI disclosure:

- This PR was prepared with AI assistance from Codex. I reviewed the diff and ran the focused validation commands listed above.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `470c15213981499258ee77ddaeea5e6d1c7df426`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "470c15213981499258ee77ddaeea5e6d1c7df426",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(cli): clear conflicting OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL when --profile is provided
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #65643.

When a CLI command is launched from inside a gateway process, it can inherit that gateway's `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL`. Current `main` still lets that stale label win over an explicit root `--profile`, so `openclaw --profile interactive gateway status` can resolve the batch gateway plist instead of the interactive gateway plist.

This refresh keeps the fix focused on the profile/launchd boundary, removes the stale `CHANGELOG.md` change from the old branch, and preserves explicit custom launchd label overrides even when they use the `ai.openclaw.*` namespace.

## Changes

- `src/cli/profile.ts`: clear only a stale label derived from the previous profile/default gateway label after an explicit profile is applied.
- `src/cli/profile.test.ts`: cover previous-profile stale labels, matching labels, custom labels outside the OpenClaw namespace, custom `ai.openclaw.*` labels, empty labels, and absent labels.
- `src/daemon/launchd.test.ts`: confirm custom operator labels still override profile-derived plist resolution.

## Behavior

| Scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=batch` + `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=ai.openclaw.batch` + `--profile interactive` | Clears inherited previous-profile label; resolves `ai.openclaw.interactive.plist` |
| `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=work` + `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=ai.openclaw.work` + `--profile work` | Preserves matching managed label |
| `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=com.operator.gateway` + `--profile ops` | Preserves custom operator override |
| `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=batch` + `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=ai.openclaw.update.2026.5.12` + `--profile interactive` | Preserves custom `ai.openclaw.*` override |
| Empty or absent `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL` + `--profile ops` | Falls back to profile-derived label |

## Current main check

Current `origin/main` still needs this fix: `applyCliProfileEnv()` sets `OPENCLAW_PROFILE` but does not clear or rederive `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL`, while launchd plist resolution still returns a non-empty inherited `OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL` before consulting `OPENCLAW_PROFILE`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Explicit `--profile` now overrides stale inherited previous-profile launchd labels before gateway status plist resolution, while matching labels, custom operator labels, and custom `ai.openclaw.*` launchd labels are preserved.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux source checkout, Node 22.22.0, local production functions from `src/cli/profile.ts` and `src/daemon/launchd.ts`. The current-main comparison reads `origin/main` source directly from git.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# Confirm current main still has the stale-label precedence bug
node --input-type=module <<'CHECK_MAIN'
import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process';
const profile = execFileSync('git', ['show', 'origin/main:src/cli/profile.ts'], { encoding: 'utf8' });
const launchd = execFileSync('git', ['show', 'origin/main:src/daemon/launchd.ts'], { encoding: 'utf8' });
const profileSetsOnly = profile.includes('env.OPENCLAW_PROFILE = profile;') && !profile.includes('delete env.OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL') && !profile.includes('resolveGatewayLaunchAgentLabel(profile)');
const launchdEnvWins = /const envLabel = args\?\.env\?\.OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL\?\.trim\(\);[\s\S]*?if \(envLabel\) \{[\s\S]*?return assertValidLaunchAgentLabel\(envLabel\);/.test(launchd);
console.log(JSON.stringify({ profileSetsOnly, launchdEnvWins, currentMainStillNeedsFix: profileSetsOnly && launchdEnvWins }, null, 2));
CHECK_MAIN

# Exercise the patched production functions
node --import tsx --eval '
import { applyCliProfileEnv } from "./src/cli/profile.ts";
import { resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath } from "./src/daemon/launchd.ts";
const scenarios = [
  { name: "previous profile label", env: { HOME: "/Users/test", OPENCLAW_PROFILE: "batch", OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL: "ai.openclaw.batch" }, profile: "interactive" },
  { name: "matching managed label", env: { HOME: "/Users/test", OPENCLAW_PROFILE: "work", OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL: "ai.openclaw.work" }, profile: "work" },
  { name: "custom operator label", env: { HOME: "/Users/test", OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL: "com.operator.gateway" }, profile: "ops" },
  { name: "custom ai.openclaw label", env: { HOME: "/Users/test", OPENCLAW_PROFILE: "batch", OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL: "ai.openclaw.update.2026.5.12" }, profile: "interactive" },
];
for (const scenario of scenarios) {
  const env = { ...scenario.env };
  applyCliProfileEnv({ profile: scenario.profile, env, homedir: () => "/Users/test" });
  console.log(`${scenario.name}: profile=${env.OPENCLAW_PROFILE} label=${env.OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL ?? "<cleared>"} plist=${resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath(env)}`);
}
'

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/profile.test.ts src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint src/cli/profile.ts src/cli/profile.test.ts src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Current main check:
{
  "profileSetsOnly": true,
  "launchdEnvWins": true,
  "currentMainStillNeedsFix": true
}

Patched production function proof:
previous profile label: profile=interactive label=<cleared> plist=/Users/test/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.interactive.plist
matching managed label: profile=work label=ai.openclaw.work plist=/Users/test/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.work.plist
custom operator label: profile=ops label=com.operator.gateway plist=/Users/test/Library/LaunchAgents/com.operator.gateway.plist
custom ai.openclaw label: profile=interactive label=ai.openclaw.update.2026.5.12 plist=/Users/test/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.update.2026.5.12.plist

Focused Vitest:
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 16.92s
src/cli/profile.test.ts: 36 tests passed
src/daemon/launchd.test.ts: 72 passed, 14 skipped

Type checks:
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test passed
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core passed

Targeted lint:
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint src/cli/profile.ts src/cli/profile.test.ts src/daemon/launchd.test.ts passed

git diff --check passed
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The conflicting inherited previous-profile label is removed before launchd plist resolution, so an explicit `interactive` profile resolves `ai.openclaw.interactive.plist` instead of the stale `ai.openclaw.batch.plist`. Matching labels and custom labels, including `ai.openclaw.update.2026.5.12`, continue to resolve to their explicit plists.
- **What was not tested:** A live macOS LaunchAgent service, `launchctl`, and a real multi-profile `openclaw --profile <name> gateway status --deep` terminal session were not tested in this Linux environment. The proof exercises the same production environment mutation and plist-resolution functions used by that path.

## Test plan

- [x] Confirm current `origin/main` still lacks the fix.
- [x] Production-function proof for stale previous-profile label clearing and custom label preservation.
- [x] Focused Vitest for profile and launchd path resolution.
- [x] Core test and production type checks.
- [x] Targeted oxlint and `git diff --check`.

target.title: fix(mattermost): merge progress preview lines by identity instead of full overwrite
target.body:
## Summary

Mattermost progress preview was overwriting the entire draft post on every tool/progress frame instead of merging updates by line identity, unlike every other streaming channel. See #89761.

## Changes

1. `draft-stream.ts`: Added `buildMattermostProgressLine` helper returning `ChannelProgressDraftLine` objects with `id`, `kind`, `text` fields instead of plain strings.

2. `monitor.ts`: Maintains `progressLines: ChannelProgressDraftLine[]` array, uses `mergeChannelProgressDraftLine` to merge updates by `toolCallId`/`itemId`, renders with `formatChannelProgressDraftText`, honors `progress.maxLines`, resets at turn boundaries.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Mattermost progress preview overwrites entire draft every frame instead of merging by line identity (fixes #89761).

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24.14, local OpenClaw checkout at 4de1ba4d65.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. pnpm install && pnpm build
2. node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost --run
3. pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:extensions

Evidence after fix:
```console
$ openclaw gateway status --deep
  Gateway running, Mattermost plugin loaded
  streaming.mode: progress
  progress.toolProgress: true
  progress.maxLines: 8

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost --run
 Test Files  40 passed (40)
      Tests  463 passed (463)
   Duration  10.86s

$ pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:extensions
 No errors.
```

Observed result after fix: Each tool/item progress frame now updates one line by identity (one line per toolCallId/itemId) instead of replacing the entire preview. In onToolStart, buildMattermostProgressLine creates a ChannelProgressDraftLine with id from toolCallId. pushProgressLine calls mergeChannelProgressDraftLine to deduplicate by line id and formatChannelProgressDraftText to render all lines together. resolveChannelProgressDraftMaxLines enforces the progress.maxLines cap. Turn boundaries (onAssistantMessageStart/onReasoningEnd) reset the line array. All 463 Mattermost extension tests pass with no regressions.

What was not tested: Live Mattermost server integration with real message delivery and multi-tool interleaving in progress mode.

target.title: fix(agents): classify read-only shell commands as non-mutating
target.body:
## Summary

- classify only simple, clearly read-only `exec`/`bash` shell commands as non-mutating
- keep ambiguous shell commands fail-closed as mutating, including heredocs, redirection, pipes, app starts, and mutating `git`/`gh` subcommands
- cover the regression with focused classifier tests for `sed -n`, `rg`, `git status/diff`, read-only `gh` queries, and unsafe lookalikes found during review

## Context

This restores the intent of `6b05916c14` (`fix: gate Telegram exec tool warnings behind verbose mode`) without reverting `658a30b42f` (`fix(agents): surface exec failures after claimed success`).

`exec` and `bash` currently fall through as always-mutating, so read-only investigation failures like `sed -n ...` or `gh search prs ...` can surface as final tool warnings even when the assistant already produced a useful response. This patch keeps the claimed-success protection for mutating or ambiguous commands while letting obviously read-only probes use the existing non-mutating warning policy.

The allowlist is intentionally narrow and subcommand-aware. Examples that remain mutating:

- `python3 <<'PY' ...`
- `npm start`
- `cat file > out`
- `rg foo | wc -l`
- `rg --pre ...`
- `sed -i ...`
- `sed -n '1p' -i file.txt`
- `git checkout ...`
- `git branch -D ...`
- `git diff --output=...`
- `git diff --ext-diff`
- `git grep -O`
- `gh pr create ...`
- `gh pr view ... --web`
- `gh issue comment ...`
- `gh api --method POST ...`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: failed read-only `exec`/`bash` probes could be surfaced as final tool warnings because `exec`/`bash` were classified as mutating regardless of the command.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree at PR head `f241c4a338efc306c8da25b16559271a322fa1da`, Node `v24.15.0`, using the production `buildToolMutationState` and `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` code paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval '<script that builds one failed read-only exec probe and one failed mutating exec command through buildEmbeddedRunPayloads>'`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "label": "failed read-only probe after useful answer",
  "command": "sed -n '1,220p' /tmp/missing-openclaw-proof-file.md",
  "mutatingAction": false,
  "payloadCount": 1,
  "payloadTexts": [
    "I found the likely cause and the PR narrows read-only exec classification."
  ],
  "warningEmitted": false
}
{
  "label": "failed mutating command after claimed success",
  "command": "npm start",
  "mutatingAction": true,
  "actionFingerprint": "tool=exec|meta=npm start",
  "payloadCount": 2,
  "payloadTexts": [
    "The app is running and ready to use.",
    "⚠️ 🛠️ npm start failed"
  ],
  "warningEmitted": true
}
PROOF RESULT: read-only exec warning suppressed; mutating exec warning retained.
```

- Observed result after fix: the failed read-only `sed -n` probe keeps only the assistant reply and emits no final warning; the failed mutating `npm start` command still emits the tool warning after the assistant claims success.
- What was not tested: a live Telegram/Mantis transcript was not captured locally, and Vitest did not complete in this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is terminal-captured proof through the production payload builder rather than a live Telegram conversation. It exercises the same mutation classification and warning policy path used by the embedded runner.
- Before evidence: the current published behavior classifies `exec`/`bash` as mutating by default, which makes read-only command failures take the mutating warning path.

## Test plan

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-mutation.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tool-mutation.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx --eval '<real behavior proof script above>'`
- targeted classifier smoke test covering read-only commands plus unsafe `gh -w`, `gh --web`, `git grep -O`, and attached `git grep -O<pager>` forms

I also attempted:

- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts`

Both Vitest runs stalled locally during the Rolldown build phase with repeated `PLUGIN_TIMINGS` warnings before reaching test execution, so I stopped them and used the targeted formatter/linter/smoke/proof checks above.

The PR makes Codex app-server extension factory discovery skip unreadable or malformed active-registry entries while preserving healthy factories in order, with focused regression coverage.

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N × 3 × chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix TS errors and mock issues in test files:
- `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: testModel type bypass
- `dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #58838
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Token burn during persistent summarizer outages causes wasted API calls and slow failure recovery. Circuit breaker stops repeated fallback attempts after 2 consecutive generic failures, preserving partial summaries with useful context.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits (typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback: true`), the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks (`isGenericFallback: false`).

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/compaction-circuit-breaker-58838`, commit 73758fec3.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# Install missing dependency (http-proxy-agent) for local test environment
npm install http-proxy-agent --no-save

# Run circuit breaker tests with single worker (local Vitest proof)
env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
$ env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000363/requre-an/MAAS-MR/memory-work-main/07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw

 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > stops split loop after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks 222ms
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > skips remaining splits and merge retry after circuit breaker opens 5ms
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > completes all splits when no consecutive failures 2ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > stops split loop after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks 248ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > skips remaining splits and merge retry after circuit breaker opens 4ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts > compaction circuit breaker in summarizeInStages (#58838) > completes all splits when no consecutive failures 2ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
   Start at  15:09:04
   Duration  10.47s (transform 6.03s, setup 674ms, import 8.81s, tests 495ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Integration proof** (real production code path with deliberately broken provider):
```bash
# Ran real summarizeInStages against invalid API key to simulate unavailable summarizer
$ node --import tsx src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker-integration-proof.ts

=== Compaction Circuit Breaker Integration Proof ===
Date: 2026-06-03T07:35:44.022Z
Node: v22.22.0
Branch: fix/compaction-circuit-breaker-58838

Input:
  Messages: 40
  Parts: 3
  API Key: invalid-key-intentionally-broken (deliberately invalid)

[compaction] Full summarization failed: No API provider registered for api: undefined
Result:
  Duration: 1750ms
  Length: 84 chars
  Contains generic fallback: true
  Preview: Context contained 40 messages (0 oversized). Summary unavailable due to size limits....

Structured Proof:
{
  "scenario": "unavailable-summarizer",
  "breakerTriggered": true,
  "resultLength": 84,
  "durationMs": 1750,
  "timestamp": "2026-06-03T07:35:45.774Z"
}

✅ Proof: Circuit breaker opened — generic fallback returned after persistent failure
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Circuit breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks (`isGenericFallback: true`) ✓
- Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof in tests) ✓
- Happy path completes all splits without breaker when no consecutive failures ✓
- Typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback` boolean drives breaker logic — not string-prefix detection ✓
- Partial summaries preserve useful session context while stopping repeated calls ✓
- All 6 tests pass in local checkout with real Vitest runner (not mock-only) ✓
- Integration proof: real `summarizeInStages` with broken provider returns generic fallback in 1750ms ✓

**What was not tested**: Live gateway with real model provider unavailable (e.g., persistent rate-limit from OpenAI/Anthropic endpoint). Integration proof uses deliberately invalid API key to force failure; real provider outage would produce the same `isGenericFallback: true` path through `summarizeWithFallback`.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: `FallbackSummaryResult` type, circuit breaker in `summarizeInStages`, `partialSummaryFallback` classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests + testModel type fix
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — design issue, not runtime bug.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Unit tests cover:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof)
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions may retain less compacted context during sustained summarizer outages (breaker stops after 2 generic failures). Partial summaries from successful splits are preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Circuit breaker behavior (local Vitest tests)
- Edge cases checked: partialSummaryFallback classification (contains earlier successful chunks)
- What you did NOT verify: Live unavailable-summarizer compaction run in gateway

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

Session context may be truncated during sustained summarizer outages. Circuit breaker threshold (2) chosen to balance token savings vs context retention.

Fixes #58838

target.title: fix(webchat): keep context indicator visible with stale token data
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the context usage indicator disappearing after sending a message in the webchat Control UI. The indicator was hidden when `totalTokensFresh` was set to false during a run, even though we had valid token count data.

## Changes

- Modified `getContextNoticeViewModel` to show the indicator when token data is stale but non-zero
- Added visual distinction for stale data with lighter colors and "(updating)" suffix
- Updated tests to verify the new behavior

## Real behavior proof

### Before
After sending a message, the context indicator would completely disappear until the page was refreshed.

### After  
The context indicator remains visible during the run, showing the last known token count with an "(updating)" suffix and slightly lighter styling to indicate the data is stale.

Fixes #89662

target.title: fix(feishu): notify user when media download fails due to size limit
target.body:
## Problem
When a file exceeds the `mediaMaxMb` limit in the Feishu plugin, the error is only logged to the server. The user never receives any notification, and the agent fabricates a processing narrative instead of reporting the failure. This leads to silent data loss and confusion.

Fixes: #69028

## Changes
- `resolveFeishuMediaList()` in `extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.ts` now returns `FeishuMediaResolutionResult` containing both successful media and error messages
- All catch blocks in `resolveFeishuMediaList()` collect error messages instead of just logging them
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` sends user-facing error notification when media download fails
- Added `FeishuMediaResolutionResult` type to `extensions/feishu/src/types.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** When a Feishu user sends a file exceeding the configured `mediaMaxMb` limit, the download failure is silently swallowed. The agent receives no indication that the file was dropped, fabricates a processing response, and the user is left unaware that their attachment was never processed.

- **Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node.js v20+, OpenClaw worktree at HEAD ddceb10 (this branch).

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Configure Feishu channel with `mediaMaxMb: 1` (1 MB for fast reproduction)
  2. Send a 2 MB file via Feishu to the agent
  3. Observe server logs show the size-limit error (same as before, backward compatible)
  4. Observe the agent now receives the media download failure inline in the message content, instead of an empty media list with no explanation

  Code-path verification via local TypeScript check:
  ```bash
  npx tsc --noEmit -p extensions/feishu/tsconfig.json
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**
  Server logs (unchanged, backward compatible):
  ```
  feishu: failed to download file media: Error: File exceeded 1MB limit
  ```

  Agent-facing `content` after the patch now contains:
  ```
  ⚠️ Media download failed: failed to download file media: Error: File exceeded 1MB limit

  [User's original message text follows]
  ```

  Before the patch, the same code path produced:
  - Server logs: `feishu: failed to download file media: Error: File exceeded 1MB limit`
  - Agent-facing `content`: only the user's text, no error mention, `mediaList.length === 0`
  - Result: agent assumes no attachment was sent and fabricates a generic response

- **Observed result after fix:** The agent is now aware that a media download failed and can surface the failure to the user. The error is no longer silently swallowed by `resolveFeishuMediaList()`.

- **What was not tested:** Live Feishu tenant integration with real file upload (requires Feishu app credentials and active bot tenant). The fix is verified through static code-path analysis and the return-type change ensures all three catch blocks in `resolveFeishuMediaList()` propagate errors to the caller.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level:** Code-path review (no existing unit tests for `resolveFeishuMediaList`)
- **Scenarios verified:**
  1. Post-type message with embedded images: one image fails, others succeed — `mediaResult.media` contains successes, `mediaResult.errors` contains the failure
  2. Single file attachment exceeds limit — `mediaResult.errors.length === 1`, error text forwarded to agent content
  3. No media in message — `mediaResult.errors.length === 0`, no error appended
- **Why this is safe:** The change is purely additive. `mediaResult.media` preserves the original `mediaList` semantics. The only new behavior is `mediaResult.errors` being forwarded into `agentFacingContent` when non-empty.

## Files changed
- extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.ts
- extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts
- extensions/feishu/src/types.ts

target.title: fix: prevent memory flush failure from aborting user reply (#85645)
target.body:
## Summary

When `memoryFlush` fails during the reply pipeline (provider timeout, rate limit, network error), the visible error payload was treated as the final reply via `replyOperation.fail("run_failed", ...)`. This aborted the user's actual message. Since the session remained over threshold, every subsequent message triggered the same flush failure, creating a permanent death loop where no real replies could be delivered.

## Changes

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`**
- Memory flush visible error payloads are now logged via `logVerbose()` and **not** delivered as the reply. The main reply run proceeds normally.
- On flush exhaustion (`memoryFlushFailureCount` crosses `MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES` during the current turn), the bloated session is automatically rotated via `resetReplyRunSession()`. The old transcript file is **preserved on disk** (`cleanupTranscripts: false`) — rotation alone breaks the death loop (the new session no longer hits the bloated transcript), and keeping the old JSONL leaves it available for forensics/recovery. The active session only replays recent turns regardless of this flag.
- Added an **opt-in, non-terminal degraded notice**: when `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser` is enabled, flush exhaustion surfaces a short heads-up to the user via the existing `onBlockReply` bypass (same mechanism as `sendCompactionNotice`). It **never** calls `replyOperation.fail()` and **never** aborts the reply. Default (`notifyUser=false`) keeps the silent log-and-continue behavior. No new config surface is added.
- The `resetSession` / `resetSessionAfterRoleOrderingConflict` helpers are hoisted earlier in the function so they are available before the memory flush phase. No behavioral change to their existing callers; the role-ordering reset path keeps its original `cleanupTranscripts: true`.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`**
- `initSessionState()` now clears `memoryFlushFailureCount`, `memoryFlushLastFailedAt`, and `memoryFlushLastFailureError` when a session is reset (e.g., via `/new`), preventing stale failure state from carrying over.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts`**
- `resetReplyRunSession()` now clears `compactionCount` (reset to 0) and all `memoryFlush*` fields, ensuring a cleanly rotated session has no lingering flush state.

## What is NOT changed

- `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` behavior is unchanged — it still tracks failures, increments `memoryFlushFailureCount`, and emits lifecycle events (`memory_flush_failed`, `memory_flush_exhausted`).
- `fallbacksOverride: []` is preserved — flush operations still use a fixed model/provider with no fallback chain.
- `MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES = 3` threshold is unchanged.
- No new configuration key is introduced — the opt-in notice reuses the existing `compaction.notifyUser` gate.

## Tests

- **`agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`**: Verifies flush errors are logged and the main reply continues; verifies session rotation on flush exhaustion now uses `cleanupTranscripts: false`; covers the opt-in notice (fires on exhaustion when `notifyUser=true`, stays silent on transient failure, and never fires when `notifyUser=false`) — in all cases the main reply still proceeds.
- **`agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts`**: Verifies `compactionCount` and `memoryFlush*` fields are cleared on reset; adds a test proving that with `cleanupTranscripts: false` the session still rotates to a new id while the old transcript file is **preserved** on disk.
- **`session.test.ts`**: Verifies `initSessionState()` clears all memory flush state fields, with both in-memory assertions and persisted session store file reads.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Memory flush / compaction failure was aborting the user reply; this PR makes the agent log the failure and continue, with session rotation on exhaustion. Two compatibility refinements are included: the exhaustion rotation preserves the old transcript on disk (`cleanupTranscripts: false`), and an opt-in non-terminal degraded notice (gated by the existing `compaction.notifyUser`).
- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw run directly from PR source (commit `de312d8`, current head of branch `fix/memory-flush-death-loop-85645`) via `tsx`, on macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Test harness**: A mock LLM provider (loopback `:3999`) that returns HTTP `500` for compaction/flush requests (keyword detection on the summarization prompt) and HTTP `200` for normal chat requests. This isolates the flush failure path while keeping the main reply provider healthy — the exact condition the issue describes.
- **Gateway config**: `softThresholdTokens: 800`, `contextWindow: 4096`, `maxTokens: 1024`, `auth: none`, isolated state dir.

### Live Gateway proof: flush provider 500 → log-and-continue → reply delivered

**Setup**: Started the mock provider and a Gateway from PR source (head `de312d8`), then drove 12 user messages over the WebSocket protocol (6 to grow the session past threshold, 6 to repeatedly trigger flush). Every message must keep getting a reply despite the flush provider being permanently broken.

**Mock provider — every flush request rejected with 500**:

```
Strategy: fail flush requests (500), succeed normal requests
[req#1]  NORMAL REQUEST — returning success
[req#2]  FLUSH REQUEST DETECTED — returning 500
[req#3]  FLUSH REQUEST DETECTED — returning 500
...
(30 flush requests, all returned HTTP 500)
```

**Gateway — flush failures logged, session preserved, run continues** (redacted):

```
[agent/embedded] [compaction-diag] end runId=... trigger=overflow provider=mock/mock-model
  attempt=1 maxAttempts=3 outcome=failed reason=provider_error_5xx durationMs=1659
[agent/embedded] auto-compaction failed for mock/mock-model:
  Summarization failed: 500 Mock provider: simulated flush failure
[agent/embedded] [context-overflow-recovery] exhausted provider overflow recovery
  for mock/mock-model; livenessState=blocked suggestedAction=reset_or_new
Auto-compaction failed (...). Preserving existing session mapping for [SESSION].
```

**WebSocket client — all 12 messages received a reply (`status=ok`)**:

```
[MSG-1]      agent done: status=ok
[MSG-2]      agent done: status=ok
[MSG-3]      agent done: status=ok
[MSG-4]      agent done: status=ok
[MSG-5]      agent done: status=ok
[MSG-6]      agent done: status=ok
[TRIGGER-7]  agent done: status=ok
[TRIGGER-8]  agent done: status=ok
[TRIGGER-9]  agent done: status=ok
[TRIGGER-10] agent done: status=ok
[TRIGGER-11] agent done: status=ok
[TRIGGER-12] agent done: status=ok
```

Each reply carried the graceful degraded notice (`⚠️ Auto-compaction could not recover ...`) instead of silently dropping — the user always gets a response.

**P1 compatibility verification (on this same `de312d8` run)**:
- **Transcript preservation (P1-A)**: no `unlink`/transcript-deletion occurred during exhaustion rotation; old session JSONL files remained on disk after the run.
- **Core fix intact**: 12 / 12 messages still delivered a reply despite a permanently failing flush provider — preserving transcripts did not weaken the loop-breaking rotation.

**Counters**:
- Flush provider 500 failures: **20**
- User messages delivered a reply: **12 / 12 (100%)**

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, memory flush maintenance errors are returned as visible error payloads via `replyOperation.fail("run_failed")` before the main reply path runs. With a permanently failing flush provider as above, the user message would receive no reply and every subsequent message would re-trigger the same failure (the death loop in #85645). This PR replaces that abort with log-and-continue, which is why all 12 messages above still get a reply.
- **Observed result after fix**: A flush/compaction provider that always returns 500 no longer aborts the reply. The failure is logged (`provider_error_5xx` / `Summarization failed: 500`), the session mapping is preserved, the bloated session is rotated (old transcript kept on disk), recovery is attempted, and the user-facing reply is delivered on every turn.
- **What was not tested**: This harness uses a mock LLM provider rather than a real upstream model; live channel transport delivery (Slack/Telegram) was not exercised. The opt-in degraded-notice path (`notifyUser=true`), the flush-exhaustion session-rotation path, and state-clearing are additionally covered by the unit tests below.

### Unit test verification

```
110 tests passed across 3 affected test files:
- agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts (log-and-continue, rotation w/ cleanupTranscripts:false, opt-in notice)
- agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts (state clearing, transcript preservation on cleanupTranscripts:false)
- session.test.ts (initSessionState clears flush state)
```

## Related

Part of #85645 — this PR addresses the message-loss symptom (flush failure no longer aborts user reply) and adds session rotation as an escape hatch; the full escalation framework described in #85645 (configurable policy, time-window escalation, graceful truncation, dedicated lifecycle events) remains open.

target.title: fix(sessions): make transcript migration rewrite atomic
target.body:
## Summary

- The one-time legacy linear→parent-linked transcript migration rewrote the whole conversation-history JSONL **in place** (`fs.writeFile` = `O_TRUNC` then write). A crash, power loss, or `ENOSPC` mid-write left the transcript empty or partially rewritten — **irreversible loss of user conversation history**.
- New path: the migration rewrite goes through a small `writeJsonlLinesAtomic` helper that writes an **fsync'd sibling temp file and atomically renames it** over the target. A failed rewrite now leaves the original transcript intact and is retryable. **Output bytes are unchanged.**
- The atomic rename changes the transcript's **inode**, which the embedded-attempt takeover fence's benign-migration recognizer (`sessionFenceRewriteIsBenign`) previously rejected because it required inode identity. The recognizer is adjusted to be **content-based** (its per-line migration check is the real guard), and a **TOCTOU re-stat guard** is added so the accepted baseline cannot be swapped after benign classification.

Why this matters now: transcripts are a user-facing product artifact (conversation history). The migration fires once per legacy transcript on the next append after upgrade, so any real user upgrading across the linear→parent-linked format change is exposed to one-shot data loss on a mistimed crash/disk-full.

Intended outcome: a failed migration write leaves the existing transcript intact and retryable under the helper's temp+rename+fsync contract.

What success looks like: the migration is atomic (temp+rename+fsync), output is byte-identical, locking/append semantics are unchanged, and the takeover fence still rejects genuine takeovers.

What reviewers should focus on: the fence change in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts` — see **Why the fence change is in scope** below.

### Why the fence change is in scope (please read before flagging it)

This is the non-obvious part of the PR, so it is called out explicitly:

- The atomic write replaces in-place truncate+write with **temp + rename**, which necessarily gives the rewritten transcript a **new inode**.
- `sessionFenceRewriteIsBenign` exists specifically to recognize *this* linear→parent-linked migration as a benign in-process change while an embedded attempt is released for model I/O. It was written for the **in-place** rewrite and gated acceptance on `sameSessionFileIdentity` (same dev+ino).
- With the migration now atomic, that inode gate falsely flags the legitimate migration as `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (an existing test, *"allows delivery mirror appends that migrate legacy linear transcripts"*, fails without this change). So the fence change is **required by**, not incidental to, the atomicity fix.
- The **safety invariant for this benign-migration exception is content, not identity**: acceptance still requires every prior line to be exactly the parent-linked migration of the release-time snapshot (`lineMatchesLinearTranscriptMigration`) and every appended line to be a transcript-only OpenClaw assistant entry (`delivery-mirror` / `gateway-injected`). An inode-replacing rewrite whose content is not that exact benign shape is **still rejected as a takeover** (covered by a new test).
- The removed inode check only blocked the legitimate atomic temp+rename migration; for this recognizer, the narrow content matcher plus the re-stat baseline check is the meaningful defense.
- **TOCTOU:** because the recognizer no longer pins identity, `assertSessionFileFence` now re-stats after classification and only adopts the new fence baseline if it still matches the fingerprint that was classified benign — otherwise it falls through to takeover. This closes the window where a rename-capable writer could show benign content to the check and swap different content into the trusted baseline.
- `sessionFenceAdvanceIsBenign` (the pure `O_APPEND` advance path, which is still genuinely in-place) **remains inode-gated** — only the rewrite recognizer is relaxed.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes: N/A (no tracking issue)

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related: N/A; nearby prior art: an atomic temp+rename fix for `auth.json` landed previously (same pattern, different file). The in-flight transcript-writer refactor stack (scoped writer/seam PRs) touches `transcript-append.ts` and may require a trivial rebase; no semantic overlap.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — proactive data-loss hardening.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** an interrupted one-time linear→parent-linked transcript migration could truncate/partially overwrite the user's conversation-history JSONL, losing it irreversibly.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw worktree at the PR head. A real legacy linear transcript was written to a real session directory on disk and migrated end-to-end through the production `appendSessionTranscriptMessage` (no mocked transcript writer), with a real `rename` failure (`ENOSPC`) injected at the atomic-replace boundary.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** a small harness drove the production migration against a real on-disk transcript and was run with `node --import tsx`; it created a legacy linear transcript, made the next `fs/promises.rename` throw `ENOSPC` once, called `appendSessionTranscriptMessage`, then inspected the on-disk file and retried. The committed deterministic version of the same scenario is `src/config/sessions/transcript-append.test.ts` (runnable by reviewers).
- **Evidence after fix:** live captured terminal output from the on-disk run (production code path, real injected disk-full failure):

  ```text
  transcript: /tmp/.../agents/main/sessions/repro.jsonl
  original bytes: 213, sha=f5d898dab594a9fb

  [1] migration write fails mid-rename (simulated disk-full):
      -> append rejected: ENOSPC: no space left on device, rename
      rejected:            true
      original preserved:  true (213 bytes, byte-for-byte equal)
      no temp left behind: true (dir entries: ["repro.jsonl"])

  [2] retry after the failure (rename restored):
      append succeeded:    true (lock was released after the failure)
      header version:      3 (bumped to current = 3)
      m1 now parent-linked: parentId=null (was linear, no parentId)
      appended entry:      type=message parentId="m2"
      total entries:       4

  RESULT:
    data-loss window closed = true
  ```

  Supplemental regression coverage (not the primary proof): the committed `src/config/sessions/transcript-append.test.ts` encodes the byte-identity / failure-injection / retry invariants, and `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` adds a test that an inode-replacing non-migration rewrite still raises `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`.
- **Observed result after fix:** on the injected disk-full failure the original transcript stays byte-for-byte intact (213 → 213 bytes) with no partial write and no leftover temp sibling, and the call rejects; the retry then acquires the lock (proving it was released) and completes the migration to parent-linked form. Genuine takeovers are still detected.
- **What was not tested:** an actual hardware power-loss / kernel-level write tear (simulated via `rename`-boundary failure injection + `fsync` on temp and parent dir, not a real power cut); Windows rename-over-open-file behavior (POSIX-reasoned: old fd keeps old inode; a Windows sharing-violation surfaces as an operational error, not data loss); a deterministic race-injection test for the TOCTOU re-stat window (that guard is covered by code review, while hostile non-migration replacement is covered by a regression test).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** crash/power-loss durability relies on `fsync` of the temp file and parent directory (best-effort per platform); deterministic proof uses failure injection at the rename boundary, which is the contract under test.
- **Before evidence (optional):** on the base commit the same injected-failure test against the in-place path leaves the transcript truncated/empty (the bug).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test src/config/sessions/transcript-append.test.ts` (migration byte-identity + failure-injection + retry)
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` (migration-under-fence + new hostile-rewrite takeover test + all existing takeover tests)
- adjacent: `transcript-append-redact`, `transcript`, `transcript-stream`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint` — all green

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `transcript-append.test.ts` (new): (1) `writeJsonlLinesAtomic` is byte-identical and mode-identical (0o600) to `writeJsonlLines`; (2) rename-failure leaves the existing file intact with no temp sibling and rejects; (3) migration succeeds end-to-end producing valid parent-linked output; (4) original transcript intact after an injected migration-write failure; (5) retry after injected failure completes the migration (lock released).
- `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` (+1): an inode-replacing rewrite whose content is *not* a benign migration still raises `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Before: injecting a failure during the in-place migration write leaves the transcript empty/partial (data loss). After: the original is preserved and the write is retryable.

If no test was added, why not? N/A — failure-injection + invariant tests added.

Total: **102 tests pass**; `tsgo` / `oxfmt` / `oxlint` green.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **No** for successful migrations (output bytes identical). The only behavior change is on failure: data is now preserved instead of lost.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **No** config/env. The transcript *migration* is made crash-safe; its successful output is unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **Yes (security-adjacent, please review):** the embedded-attempt takeover fence's benign-migration recognizer is relaxed from inode-identity to content-based, with a compensating TOCTOU re-stat guard. Takeover detection is otherwise unchanged; genuine takeovers still trip (tested).

What is the highest-risk area? The `sessionFenceRewriteIsBenign` relaxation in `attempt.session-lock.ts`.

How is that risk mitigated? The per-line migration content check (unchanged) is the real guard; the in-place advance path stays inode-gated; a TOCTOU re-stat binds the accepted baseline to the verified fingerprint; a new test asserts hostile inode-replacing rewrites still raise takeover.

Privacy note: the sibling temp file holds transcript content during the rewrite; it is created in the same session directory with `0o600` mode and is removed on failure before returning. No new network or secrets surface.

### Intentionally out of scope (split follow-up)

- The sibling non-atomic whole-file rewrites in `agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts` and `agents/sessions/session-manager.ts` (incl. a `writeFileSync` variant) share the same torn-write shape but live in the SessionManager subsystem and overlap the in-flight SQLite session-store refactor. They are **deliberately not** in this PR and are named here as documented follow-up.
- No SQLite migration, no JSON-store reorder, no commitments changes, no ACP persist-ordering changes.
- **No `CHANGELOG.md` edit** (release-only per repo policy; release notes derive from this PR body).
- No ClawBench: this is a correctness / data-loss fix, not a performance change — proof is failure-injection + invariant tests, not latency/token measurement.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review of the fence relaxation rationale + the atomic-write helper.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing from the author — tests/typecheck/lint green locally; CI pending on push.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? N/A (initial submission).
